questions.explanation: Standard form requires the first number to be between 1 and 10. The decimal point moves 6 places to the left from the end of the number to after the first digit, so the power of 10 is 6.
To get a number between 1 and 10, the decimal point moves 4 places to the right. A move to the right results in a negative power, so it is 7.1 x 10^-4.

questions.questionText: Write 3.9 x $10^{5}$ as an ordinary number.
questions.explanation: The power of 10 is positive 5, so move the decimal point 5 places to the right, adding zeros as placeholders. 3.9 becomes 390,000.

questions.questionText: Write 8.2 x 10^-3 as an ordinary number.
questions.explanation: The power of 10 is negative 3, so move the decimal point 3 places to the left, adding zeros as placeholders. 8.2 becomes 0.0082.

questions.questionText: Calculate (2 x $10^{5}$) x (4 x $10^{3}$), giving your answer in standard form.
questions.explanation: Multiply the numbers: 2 x 4 = 8. Add the powers of 10: 5 + 3 = 8. Combine them to get 8 x $10^{8}$.

questions.questionText: Calculate (9 x $10^{7}$) ÷ (3 x $10^{2}$), giving your answer in standard form.
questions.explanation: Divide the numbers: 9 ÷ 3 = 3. Subtract the powers of 10: 7 - 2 = 5. Combine them to get 3 x $10^{5}$.
Since the powers of 10 are the same, add the numbers: 4.1 + 2.5 = 6.6. The power of 10 remains the same, giving 6.6 x $10^{4}$.

questions.questionText: Which of these numbers is the largest?
questions.explanation: When comparing numbers in standard form, the one with the highest power of 10 is usually the largest. 3.4 x $10^{5}$ is the largest as its power of 10 is 5.

questions.questionText: A country has a population of 52 million. Write this number in standard form.
questions.explanation: 52 million is written as 52,000,000. To express this in standard form, the decimal point moves 7 places to the left, resulting in 5.2 x $10^{7}$.

questions.questionText: The diameter of a red blood cell is approximately 0.000007 metres. How is this written in standard form?
questions.explanation: The decimal point must move 6 places to the right to get the number 7. A move to the right gives a negative power. Therefore, the answer is 7 x 10^-6.

questions.questionText: Simplify (5 x 10^-2) x (6 x $10^{8}$). Give your answer in standard form.
questions.explanation: Multiply the numbers: 5 x 6 = 30. Add the powers: -2 + 8 = 6. This gives 30 x $10^{6}$. To write this in standard form, change 30 to 3 x $10^{1}$. So, (3 x $10^{1}$) x $10^{6}$ = 3 x $10^{7}$.
